title: polypodium pseudolaterale
intro: Polypodium pseudolaterale, also known as the false polypody fern, is a species of fern native to the tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific Islands. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral effects. The health benefits of Polypodium pseudolaterale include the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, as well as its use as a wound cleanser and skin tonic. In herbal preparations, the leaves of the plant are often dried and made into teas, tinctures, or infusions to harness its medicinal properties.
